 # Specstory CLI Changelog
 
-## v1.11.0 2026-02-24
+## v1.11.0 2026-03-02
 
 ### ًں“¢ Announcements
 
@@ -10,8 +10,13 @@
 
 - `specstory sync` now supports an `--only-stats` flag that skips the full local and/or cloud sync and only generates or updates `./.specstory/statistics.json`.
 
+### âڑ™ï¸ڈ Improvements
+
+- Update project directory handling for the Gemini CLI provider. Gemini CLI `v0.30.0+` changed how it names project directories under `~/.gemini/tmp/`. Previously it used a SHA256 hash of the project's absolute path (e.g., `c18385f0...`). Now it uses the project folder's basename and stores the actual project path in a `.project_root` file inside that directory. [Issue #183](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues/183)
+
 ### ًںگ› Bug Fixes
 
+- Use file system events rather than polling in Gemini CLI provider to avoid noisy logging activity (every 30s) during `specstory watch`.
 - Ensure Claude Code provider doesn't use session summary self-message as first user message when creating markdown file slug. [Contribution](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/pull/178) from @salmonumbrella.

+// matchesProjectRoot checks whether dir contains a .project_root file whose
+// content matches canonicalProjectPath (after canonicalization).
+func matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonicalProjectPath string) bool {
+	content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root"))
+	if err != nil {
+		return false
+	}
+
+	storedPath := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
+	canonicalStored, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(storedPath)
+	if err != nil {
+		slog.Warn("matchesProjectRoot: Failed to canonicalize stored path, using as-is",
+			"storedPath", storedPath, "error", err)
+		canonicalStored = storedPath
+	}
+
+	return canonicalStored == canonicalProjectPath
+}
+
+// canonicalizeProjectPath returns the canonical form of projectPath for
+// case-insensitive filesystem matching. Falls back to the original path on error.
+func canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath string) string {
+	canonical, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(projectPath)
+	if err != nil {
+		slog.Warn("canonicalizeProjectPath: Failed to canonicalize, using as-is",
+			"projectPath", projectPath, "error", err)
+		return projectPath
+	}
+	return canonical
+}
+
+// findProjectDirByBasename tries the most likely name-based directory first:
+// tmpDir/<basename>. Returns the directory path if its .project_root matches,
+// or empty string otherwise.
+func findProjectDirByBasename(tmpDir, projectPath, canonicalProjectPath string) string {
+	basename := filepath.Base(projectPath)
+	candidate := filepath.Join(tmpDir, basename)
+
+	info, err := osStat(candidate)
+	if err != nil || !info.IsDir() {
+		return ""
+	}
+
+	if matchesProjectRoot(candidate, canonicalProjectPath) {
+		slog.Info("findProjectDirByBasename: Matched project directory via basename hint",
+			"dir", candidate, "projectPath", projectPath)
+		return candidate
+	}
+
+	return ""
+}
+
+// findProjectDirByProjectRoot scans tmpDir for any subdirectory whose .project_root
+// file content matches projectPath. This supports Gemini CLI v0.30.0+ which uses
+// project-name-based directories (possibly with numeric suffixes like my-project-1).
+func findProjectDirByProjectRoot(tmpDir, projectPath string) (string, error) {
+	canonicalProjectPath := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
+
+	entries, err := os.ReadDir(tmpDir)
+	if err != nil {
+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
+	}
+
+	for _, entry := range entries {
+		if !entry.IsDir() {
+			continue
+		}
+
+		dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, entry.Name())
+		if matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonicalProjectPath) {
+			slog.Info("findProjectDirByProjectRoot: Found matching project directory via .project_root",
+				"dir", dir, "projectPath", projectPath)
+			return dir, nil
+		}
+	}
 
-	return projectDir, nil
+	return "", nil
 }
 
 // ListGeminiProjectHashes returns the list of project hash directories currently on disk.

+func TestFindProjectDirByProjectRoot(t *testing.T) {
+	tests := []struct {
+		name        string
+		projectPath string
+		setup       func(t *testing.T, tmpDir string)
+		wantMatch   bool   // true if we expect a non-empty result
+		wantSuffix  string // expected directory name suffix in the result
+	}{
+		{
+			name:        "match found via .project_root file",
+			projectPath: "/tmp/my-project",
+			setup: func(t *testing.T, tmpDir string) {
+				dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "my-project")
+				if err := os.Mkdir(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
+					t.Fatalf("failed to create dir: %v", err)
+				}
+				if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root"), []byte("/tmp/my-project\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
+					t.Fatalf("failed to write .project_root: %v", err)
+				}
+			},
+			wantMatch:  true,
+			wantSuffix: "my-project",
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "no match with different project path",
+			projectPath: "/tmp/unrelated-project",
+			setup: func(t *testing.T, tmpDir string) {
+				dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "other-project")
+				if err := os.Mkdir(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
+					t.Fatalf("failed to create dir: %v", err)
+				}
+				if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root"), []byte("/tmp/other-project\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
+					t.Fatalf("failed to write .project_root: %v", err)
+				}
+			},
+			wantMatch: false,
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "empty tmp directory",
+			projectPath: "/tmp/any-project",
+			setup:       func(t *testing.T, tmpDir string) {},
+			wantMatch:   false,
+		},
+		{
+			name:        "directory without .project_root file skipped",
+			projectPath: "/tmp/any-project",
+			setup: func(t *testing.T, tmpDir string) {
+				dir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "abcdef1234567890")
+				if err := os.Mkdir(dir, 0o755); err != nil {
+					t.Fatalf("failed to create dir: %v", err)
+				}
+			},
+			wantMatch: false,
+		},
+	}
+
+	for _, tt := range tests {
+		t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
+			testTmpDir := t.TempDir()
+			tt.setup(t, testTmpDir)
+
+			got, err := findProjectDirByProjectRoot(testTmpDir, tt.projectPath)
+			if err != nil {
+				t.Fatalf("findProjectDirByProjectRoot returned error: %v", err)
+			}
+
+			if tt.wantMatch {
+				wantDir := filepath.Join(testTmpDir, tt.wantSuffix)
+				if got != wantDir {
+					t.Fatalf("got %q, want %q", got, wantDir)
+				}
+			} else {
+				if got != "" {
+					t.Fatalf("expected empty string, got %q", got)
+				}
+			}
+		})
+	}
+}
+
+func TestResolveGeminiProjectDirViaProjectRoot(t *testing.T) {
+	originalGetwd := osGetwd
+	originalUserHome := osUserHomeDir
+	originalStat := osStat
+	t.Cleanup(func() {
+		osGetwd = originalGetwd
+		osUserHomeDir = originalUserHome
+		osStat = originalStat
+	})
+
+	// Use a real temp dir as the project path so canonical path resolution works
+	projectPath := t.TempDir()
+
+	osGetwd = func() (string, error) {
+		return projectPath, nil
+	}
+	fakeHome := t.TempDir()
+	osUserHomeDir = func() (string, error) {
+		return fakeHome, nil
+	}
+
+	tmpDir := filepath.Join(fakeHome, ".gemini", "tmp")
+	if err := os.MkdirAll(tmpDir, 0o755); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to create tmp dir: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Create a project-name-based directory with .project_root
+	nameBasedDir := filepath.Join(tmpDir, "my-project")
+	if err := os.Mkdir(nameBasedDir, 0o755); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to create name-based dir: %v", err)
+	}
+	if err := os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(nameBasedDir, ".project_root"), []byte(projectPath+"\n"), 0o644); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("failed to write .project_root: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// ResolveGeminiProjectDir should find the project via .project_root fallback
+	got, err := ResolveGeminiProjectDir("")
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("expected fallback to find project dir, got error: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	if got != nameBasedDir {
+		t.Fatalf("got %q, want %q", got, nameBasedDir)
+	}
+}
+

 	originalGetwd := osGetwd
 	originalUserHome := osUserHomeDir

+// waitForProjectDir waits for a Gemini project directory using three strategies:
+//  1. Basename hint â€” tmpDir/<project-basename> with matching .project_root
+//  2. Hash-based â€” the legacy hash directory
+//  3. Full .project_root scan â€” handles suffixed dirs like my-project-1
+//
+// If none exist yet, it watches tmpDir for new directory creation.
+func waitForProjectDir(ctx context.Context, tmpDir, projectPath, hashDir string) (string, error) {
+	canonicalProjectPath := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
+	hashName := filepath.Base(hashDir)
+
+	// checkAll runs all three strategies and returns the first match.
+	checkAll := func() string {
+		// 1. Basename hint
+		if dir := findProjectDirByBasename(tmpDir, projectPath, canonicalProjectPath); dir != "" {
+			return dir
+		}
+		// 2. Hash-based
+		if info, err := os.Stat(hashDir); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
+			slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: hash-based project directory ready", "path", hashDir)
+			return hashDir
+		}
+		// 3. Full scan
+		if match, err := findProjectDirByProjectRoot(tmpDir, projectPath); err == nil && match != "" {
+			return match
+		}
+		return ""
+	}
+
+	// Try existing directories first
+	if dir := checkAll(); dir != "" {
+		return dir, nil
+	}
+
+	// Nothing exists yet â€” watch tmpDir for new directory creation
+	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
+	if err != nil {
+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create fsnotify watcher for project dir: %w", err)
+	}
+	defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }()
+
+	if err := watcher.Add(tmpDir); err != nil {
+		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to watch tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
+	}
+
+	slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: watching for project directory creation",
+		"tmpDir", tmpDir, "hashDir", hashName)
+
+	// Re-check after adding watcher to close the race window
+	if dir := checkAll(); dir != "" {
+		return dir, nil
+	}
+
+	for {
+		select {
+		case <-ctx.Done():
+			return "", ctx.Err()
+		case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
+			if !ok {
+				return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify events channel closed for project dir watcher")
+			}
+			if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
+				continue
+			}
+			createdName := filepath.Base(event.Name)
+			// Check if this is the hash-based directory
+			if createdName == hashName {
+				slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: hash-based project directory created", "path", event.Name)
+				return event.Name, nil
+			}
+			// Check if the new directory has a matching .project_root
+			if matchesProjectRoot(event.Name, canonicalProjectPath) {
+				slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: project directory created with matching .project_root",
+					"path", event.Name)
+				return event.Name, nil
+			}
+		case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
+			if !ok {
+				return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify errors channel closed for project dir watcher")
+			}
+			return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify watcher error for project dir: %w", err)
+		}
+	}
+}
+

+// waitForDirectoryFsnotify waits for a directory to exist using fsnotify on its parent.
+// The parent directory must already exist; if it doesn't, the function returns an error
+// instead of polling forever. This mirrors the Claude Code watcher pattern.
+func waitForDirectoryFsnotify(ctx context.Context, dir string, label string) error {
+	// Check if directory already exists
+	info, err := os.Stat(dir)
+	if err == nil && info.IsDir() {
+		slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: directory ready", "label", label, "path", dir)
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	parentDir := filepath.Dir(dir)
+	childName := filepath.Base(dir)
+
+	// Parent must exist â€” caller guarantees this via the sequential wait chain
+	if _, err := os.Stat(parentDir); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("parent directory %q does not exist for %s: %w", parentDir, label, err)
+	}
 
+	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
+	if err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create fsnotify watcher for %s: %w", label, err)
+	}
+	defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }()
+
+	if err := watcher.Add(parentDir); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("failed to watch parent directory %q for %s: %w", parentDir, label, err)
+	}
+
+	slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: watching for directory creation", "label", label, "parent", parentDir, "child", childName)
+
+	// Re-check after adding watcher to close the race window
+	info, err = os.Stat(dir)
+	if err == nil && info.IsDir() {
+		slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: directory ready", "label", label, "path", dir)
+		return nil
+	}
+
+	for {
 		select {
 		case <-ctx.Done():
 			return ctx.Err()
-		case <-time.After(backoff):
-			if backoff < maxBackoff {
-				backoff *= 2
-				if backoff > maxBackoff {
-					backoff = maxBackoff
-				}
+		case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
+			if !ok {
+				return fmt.Errorf("fsnotify events channel closed for %s", label)
+			}
+			if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && filepath.Base(event.Name) == childName {
+				slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: directory ready", "label", label, "path", dir)
+				return nil
+			}
+		case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
+			if !ok {
+				return fmt.Errorf("fsnotify errors channel closed for %s", label)
 			}
+			return fmt.Errorf("fsnotify watcher error for %s: %w", label, err)
 		}
 	}
 }

```text
    37â†’// HashProjectPath deterministically hashes an absolute project path using SHA256.
    38â†’// On case-insensitive filesystems (macOS), it resolves the path to its canonical
    39â†’// form with the correct case to ensure consistent hashing.
    40â†’func HashProjectPath(projectPath string) (string, error) {
    41â†’	if projectPath == "" {
    42â†’		return "", fmt.Errorf("project path is empty")
    43â†’	}
    44â†’
    45â†’	absPath, err := filepath.Abs(projectPath)
    46â†’	if err != nil {
    47â†’		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to resolve absolute path for %q: %w", projectPath, err)
    48â†’	}
    49â†’
    50â†’	// Resolve to canonical path with correct case (important for case-insensitive filesystems like macOS)
    51â†’	canonicalPath, err := spi.GetCanonicalPath(absPath)
    52â†’	if err != nil {
    53â†’		// If getting canonical path fails, use absPath as fallback
    54â†’		slog.Warn("HashProjectPath: Failed to get canonical path, using absolute path",
    55â†’			"absPath", absPath,
    56â†’			"error", err)
    57â†’		canonicalPath = absPath
    58â†’	} else if canonicalPath != absPath {

    60â†’		slog.Debug("HashProjectPath: Resolved path to canonical form",
    61â†’			"original", absPath,
    62â†’			"canonical", canonicalPath)
    63â†’	}
    64â†’
    65â†’	hash := sha256.Sum256([]byte(canonicalPath))
    66â†’	return hex.EncodeToString(hash[:]), nil
    67â†’}

   194â†’// matchesProjectRoot checks whether dir contains a .project_root file whose
   195â†’// content matches canonicalProjectPath (after canonicalization).
   196â†’func matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonicalProjectPath string) bool {
   197â†’	content, err := os.ReadFile(filepath.Join(dir, ".project_root"))

   213â†’// canonicalizeProjectPath returns the canonical form of projectPath for
```

```text
    65â†’func WatchGeminiProject(projectPath string, callback func(*spi.AgentChatSession)) error {
    66â†’	SetWatcherCallback(callback)
    67â†’	SetWatcherWorkspaceRoot(projectPath)
    68â†’
    69â†’	hashDir, err := GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
    70â†’	if err != nil {
    71â†’		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get gemini project dir: %w", err)
    72â†’	}
    73â†’
    74â†’	// Build the full directory chain: ~/.gemini â†’ tmp â†’ resolvedDir â†’ chats
    75â†’	// Each step's parent is guaranteed to exist by the previous step.
    76â†’	// $HOME (parent of geminiDir) always exists.
    77â†’	geminiDir := filepath.Dir(filepath.Dir(hashDir)) // ~/.gemini
    78â†’	tmpDir := filepath.Dir(hashDir)                  // ~/.gemini/tmp
    79â†’
    80â†’	watcherWg.Add(1)
    81â†’	go func() {
    82â†’		defer watcherWg.Done()
    83â†’
    84â†’		if err := waitForDirectoryFsnotify(watcherCtx, geminiDir, "Gemini root directory"); err != nil {
    85â†’			slog.Debug("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for root directory", "error", err)
    86â†’			return
    87â†’		}
    88â†’
    89â†’		if err := waitForDirectoryFsnotify(watcherCtx, tmpDir, "Gemini tmp directory"); err != nil {
    90â†’			slog.Debug("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for tmp directory", "error", err)
    91â†’			return
    92â†’		}
    93â†’
    94â†’		// Wait for either hash-based or .project_root-based project directory
    95â†’		resolvedDir, err := waitForProjectDir(watcherCtx, tmpDir, projectPath, hashDir)
    96â†’		if err != nil {
    97â†’			slog.Debug("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for project directory", "error", err)
    98â†’			return
    99â†’		}
   100â†’
   101â†’		chatsDir := filepath.Join(resolvedDir, "chats")
   102â†’		if err := waitForDirectoryFsnotify(watcherCtx, chatsDir, "Gemini chats directory"); err != nil {
   103â†’			slog.Debug("Stopped Gemini watcher while waiting for chats directory", "error", err)
   104â†’			return
   105â†’		}
   106â†’
   107â†’		if err := startChatsWatcher(chatsDir); err != nil {
   108â†’			slog.Error("Failed to start chats watcher", "error", err)
   109â†’		}
   110â†’
   111â†’		if err := startArtifactWatcher(filepath.Join(resolvedDir, "logs.json"), "logs.json"); err != nil {
   112â†’			slog.Warn("Failed to watch logs.json", "error", err)
   113â†’		}
   114â†’
   115â†’		if err := startArtifactWatcher(filepath.Join(resolvedDir, "shell_history"), "shell_history"); err != nil {
   116â†’			slog.Warn("Failed to watch shell_history", "error", err)
   117â†’		}
   118â†’	}()
   119â†’
   120â†’	return nil
   121â†’}
   122â†’
   123â†’// waitForProjectDir waits for a Gemini project directory using three strategies:
   124â†’//  1. Basename hint â€” tmpDir/<project-basename> with matching .project_root
   125â†’//  2. Hash-based â€” the legacy hash directory
   126â†’//  3. Full .project_root scan â€” handles suffixed dirs like my-project-1
   127â†’//
   128â†’// If none exist yet, it watches tmpDir for new directory creation.
   129â†’func waitForProjectDir(ctx context.Context, tmpDir, projectPath, hashDir string) (string, error) {
   130â†’	canonicalProjectPath := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
   131â†’	hashName := filepath.Base(hashDir)
   132â†’
   133â†’	// checkAll runs all three strategies and returns the first match.
   134â†’	checkAll := func() string {
   135â†’		// 1. Basename hint
   136â†’		if dir := findProjectDirByBasename(tmpDir, projectPath, canonicalProjectPath); dir != "" {
   137â†’			return dir
   138â†’		}
   139â†’		// 2. Hash-based
   140â†’		if info, err := os.Stat(hashDir); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   141â†’			slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: hash-based project directory ready", "path", hashDir)
   142â†’			return hashDir
   143â†’		}
   144â†’		// 3. Full scan
   145â†’		if match, err := findProjectDirByProjectRoot(tmpDir, projectPath); err == nil && match != "" {
   146â†’			return match
   147â†’		}
   148â†’		return ""
   149â†’	}
   150â†’
   151â†’	// Try existing directories first
   152â†’	if dir := checkAll(); dir != "" {
   153â†’		return dir, nil
   154â†’	}
   155â†’
   156â†’	// Nothing exists yet â€” watch tmpDir for new directory creation
   157â†’	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   158â†’	if err != nil {
   159â†’		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create fsnotify watcher for project dir: %w", err)
   160â†’	}
   161â†’	defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }()
   162â†’
   163â†’	if err := watcher.Add(tmpDir); err != nil {
   164â†’		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to watch tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
   165â†’	}
   166â†’
   167â†’	slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: watching for project directory creation",
   168â†’		"tmpDir", tmpDir, "hashDir", hashName)
   169â†’
   170â†’	// Re-check after adding watcher to close the race window
   171â†’	if dir := checkAll(); dir != "" {
   172â†’		return dir, nil
   173â†’	}
   174â†’
   175â†’	for {
   176â†’		select {
   177â†’		case <-ctx.Done():
   178â†’			return "", ctx.Err()
   179â†’		case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   180â†’			if !ok {
   181â†’				return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify events channel closed for project dir watcher")
   182â†’			}
   183â†’			if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   184â†’				continue
   185â†’			}
   186â†’			createdName := filepath.Base(event.Name)
   187â†’			// Check if this is the hash-based directory
   188â†’			if createdName == hashName {
   189â†’				slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: hash-based project directory created", "path", event.Name)
   190â†’				return event.Name, nil
   191â†’			}
   192â†’			// Check if the new directory has a matching .project_root
   193â†’			if matchesProjectRoot(event.Name, canonicalProjectPath) {
   194â†’				slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: project directory created with matching .project_root",
   195â†’					"path", event.Name)
   196â†’				return event.Name, nil
   197â†’			}
   198â†’		case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   199â†’			if !ok {
   200â†’				return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify errors channel closed for project dir watcher")
   201â†’			}
   202â†’			return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify watcher error for project dir: %w", err)
   203â†’		}
   204â†’	}
   205â†’}
   206â†’
   207â†’func startChatsWatcher(chatsDir string) error {
   208â†’	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   209â†’	if err != nil {
   210â†’		return err
   211â†’	}
   212â†’
   213â†’	watcherWg.Add(1)
   214â†’	go func() {
   215â†’		defer watcherWg.Done()
   216â†’		defer func() {
   217â†’			_ = watcher.Close()
   218â†’		}()
   219â†’
   220â†’		if err := watcher.Add(chatsDir); err != nil {
   221â†’			slog.Error("Failed to add chats dir to watcher", "error", err)
   222â†’			return
   223â†’		}
   224â†’
   225â†’		for {
   226â†’			select {
   227â†’			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   228â†’				return
   229â†’			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   230â†’				if !ok {
   231â†’					return
   232â†’				}
   233â†’				if strings.HasSuffix(event.Name, ".json") && (event.Has(fsnotify.Write) || event.Has(fsnotify.Create)) {
   234â†’					processSessionChange(event.Name)
   235â†’				}
   236â†’			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   237â†’				if !ok {
   238â†’					return
   239â†’				}
   240â†’				slog.Error("Watcher error", "error", err)
   241â†’			}
   242â†’		}
   243â†’	}()
   244â†’
   245â†’	return nil
   246â†’}
   247â†’
   248â†’func processSessionChange(filePath string) {
   249â†’	slog.Debug("processSessionChange: Detected session file change", "file", filePath)
   250â†’
   251â†’	// Extract short session ID from filename to find related files
   252â†’	filename := filepath.Base(filePath)
   253â†’	shortID := extractSessionIDFromFilename(filename)
   254â†’	if shortID == "" {
   255â†’		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Could not extract session ID from filename", "file", filename)
   256â†’		return
   257â†’	}
   258â†’
   259â†’	// Parse the changed file to get the full session ID
   260â†’	session, err := ParseSessionFile(filePath)
   261â†’	if err != nil {
   262â†’		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to parse session file", "file", filePath, "error", err)
   263â†’		return
   264â†’	}
   265â†’
   266â†’	fullSessionID := session.ID
   267â†’
   268â†’	// Find all files for this session to merge them
   269â†’	// Why: Gemini CLI may have created multiple files for this session
   270â†’	chatsDir := filepath.Dir(filePath)
   271â†’	entries, err := os.ReadDir(chatsDir)
   272â†’	if err != nil {
   273â†’		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to read chats directory", "dir", chatsDir, "error", err)
   274â†’		// Fall back to single-file session
   275â†’		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   276â†’		triggerCallback(agentSession)
   277â†’		return
   278â†’	}
   279â†’
   280â†’	// Collect all files that match this session's short ID
   281â†’	var sessionFiles []string
   282â†’	for _, entry := range entries {
   283â†’		if entry.IsDir() {
   284â†’			continue
   285â†’		}
   286â†’		if extractSessionIDFromFilename(entry.Name()) == shortID {
   287â†’			sessionFiles = append(sessionFiles, filepath.Join(chatsDir, entry.Name()))
   288â†’		}
   289â†’	}
   290â†’
   291â†’	slog.Debug("processSessionChange: Found session files to merge",
   292â†’		"shortID", shortID,
   293â†’		"fileCount", len(sessionFiles))
   294â†’
   295â†’	// Merge all files for this session
   296â†’	mergedSession, err := parseAndMergeSessionFiles(shortID, sessionFiles)
   297â†’	if err != nil {
   298â†’		slog.Error("processSessionChange: Failed to merge session files",
   299â†’			"sessionId", fullSessionID,
   300â†’			"fileCount", len(sessionFiles),
   301â†’			"error", err)
   302â†’		// Fall back to single-file session
   303â†’		agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(session, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   304â†’		triggerCallback(agentSession)
   305â†’		return
   306â†’	}
   307â†’
   308â†’	slog.Info("processSessionChange: Successfully processed session change",
   309â†’		"sessionId", mergedSession.ID,
   310â†’		"fileCount", len(sessionFiles),
   311â†’		"messageCount", len(mergedSession.Messages),
   312â†’		"startTime", mergedSession.StartTime,
   313â†’		"lastUpdated", mergedSession.LastUpdated)
   314â†’
   315â†’	agentSession := convertToAgentChatSession(mergedSession, getWatcherWorkspaceRoot(), getWatcherDebugRaw())
   316â†’	triggerCallback(agentSession)
   317â†’}
   318â†’
   319â†’// triggerCallback is a helper to call the watcher callback with proper locking
   320â†’func triggerCallback(agentSession *spi.AgentChatSession) {
   321â†’	watcherMutex.RLock()
   322â†’	cb := watcherCallback
   323â†’	watcherMutex.RUnlock()
   324â†’
   325â†’	if cb != nil && agentSession != nil {
   326â†’		cb(agentSession)
   327â†’	}
   328â†’}
   329â†’
   330â†’// waitForDirectoryFsnotify waits for a directory to exist using fsnotify on its parent.
   331â†’// The parent directory must already exist; if it doesn't, the function returns an error
   332â†’// instead of polling forever. This mirrors the Claude Code watcher pattern.
   333â†’func waitForDirectoryFsnotify(ctx context.Context, dir string, label string) error {
   334â†’	// Check if directory already exists
   335â†’	info, err := os.Stat(dir)
   336â†’	if err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   337â†’		slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: directory ready", "label", label, "path", dir)
   338â†’		return nil
   339â†’	}
   340â†’
   341â†’	parentDir := filepath.Dir(dir)
   342â†’	childName := filepath.Base(dir)
   343â†’
   344â†’	// Parent must exist â€” caller guarantees this via the sequential wait chain
   345â†’	if _, err := os.Stat(parentDir); err != nil {
   346â†’		return fmt.Errorf("parent directory %q does not exist for %s: %w", parentDir, label, err)
   347â†’	}
   348â†’
   349â†’	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   350â†’	if err != nil {
   351â†’		return fmt.Errorf("failed to create fsnotify watcher for %s: %w", label, err)
   352â†’	}
   353â†’	defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }()
   354â†’
   355â†’	if err := watcher.Add(parentDir); err != nil {
   356â†’		return fmt.Errorf("failed to watch parent directory %q for %s: %w", parentDir, label, err)
   357â†’	}
   358â†’
   359â†’	slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: watching for directory creation", "label", label, "parent", parentDir, "child", childName)
   360â†’
   361â†’	// Re-check after adding watcher to close the race window
   362â†’	info, err = os.Stat(dir)
   363â†’	if err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   364â†’		slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: directory ready", "label", label, "path", dir)
   365â†’		return nil
   366â†’	}
   367â†’
   368â†’	for {
   369â†’		select {
   370â†’		case <-ctx.Done():
   371â†’			return ctx.Err()
   372â†’		case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   373â†’			if !ok {
   374â†’				return fmt.Errorf("fsnotify events channel closed for %s", label)
   375â†’			}
   376â†’			if event.Has(fsnotify.Create) && filepath.Base(event.Name) == childName {
   377â†’				slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: directory ready", "label", label, "path", dir)
   378â†’				return nil
   379â†’			}
   380â†’		case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   381â†’			if !ok {
   382â†’				return fmt.Errorf("fsnotify errors channel closed for %s", label)
   383â†’			}
   384â†’			return fmt.Errorf("fsnotify watcher error for %s: %w", label, err)
   385â†’		}
   386â†’	}
   387â†’}
   388â†’
   389â†’func startArtifactWatcher(filePath string, label string) error {
   390â†’	dir := filepath.Dir(filePath)
   391â†’	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   392â†’	if err != nil {
   393â†’		return err
   394â†’	}
   395â†’
   396â†’	if err := watcher.Add(dir); err != nil {
   397â†’		_ = watcher.Close()
   398â†’		return err
   399â†’	}
   400â†’
   401â†’	watcherWg.Add(1)
   402â†’	go func() {
   403â†’		defer watcherWg.Done()
   404â†’		defer func() {
   405â†’			_ = watcher.Close()
   406â†’		}()
   407â†’
   408â†’		slog.Info("Gemini watcher: monitoring artifact", "artifact", label, "path", filePath)
   409â†’
   410â†’		for {
   411â†’			select {
   412â†’			case <-watcherCtx.Done():
   413â†’				return
   414â†’			case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   415â†’				if !ok {
   416â†’					return
   417â†’				}
   418â†’				if filepath.Clean(event.Name) != filepath.Clean(filePath) {
   419â†’					continue
   420â†’				}
   421â†’				if event.Has(fsnotify.Write) || event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   422â†’					slog.Info("Gemini artifact updated", "artifact", label, "path", event.Name, "op", event.Op.String())
   423â†’				}
   424â†’			case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   425â†’				if !ok {
   426â†’					return
   427â†’				}
   428â†’				slog.Warn("Gemini artifact watcher error", "artifact", label, "error", err)
   429â†’			}
   430â†’		}
   431â†’	}()
   432â†’
   433â†’	return nil
   434â†’}
   435â†’
```

```text
     1â†’# Specstory CLI Changelog
     2â†’
     3â†’## v1.11.0 2026-03-02
     4â†’
     5â†’### ًں“¢ Announcements
     6â†’
     7â†’- `specstory sync` now generates session statistics at `./.specstory/statistics.json`. This data is available for custom tooling or analysis, or you can safely ignore it.
     8â†’
     9â†’### ًں”§ CLI Configuration & Commands
    10â†’
    11â†’- `specstory sync` now supports an `--only-stats` flag that skips the full local and/or cloud sync and only generates or updates `./.specstory/statistics.json`.
    12â†’
    13â†’### âڑ™ï¸ڈ Improvements
    14â†’
    15â†’- Update project directory handling for the Gemini CLI provider. Gemini CLI `v0.30.0+` changed how it names project directories under `~/.gemini/tmp/`. Previously it used a SHA256 hash of the project's absolute path (e.g., `c18385f0...`). Now it uses the project folder's basename and stores the actual project path in a `.project_root` file inside that directory. [Issue #183](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/issues/183)
    16â†’
    17â†’### ًںگ› Bug Fixes
    18â†’
    19â†’- Use file system events rather than polling in Gemini CLI provider to avoid noisy logging activity (every 30s) during `specstory watch`.
    20â†’- Ensure Claude Code provider doesn't use session summary self-message as first user message when creating markdown file slug. [Contribution](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/pull/178) from @salmonumbrella.
    21â†’
    22â†’
    23â†’## v1.10.0 2026-02-23
    24â†’
    25â†’### ًں“¢ Announcements
    26â†’
    27â†’-  Added optional [OpenTelemetry](https://opentelemetry.io/) metrics for agent sessions. Enable via the `--telemetry-endpoint`, `--telemetry-service-name`, and `--no-telemetry-prompts` [flags](https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/terminal-coding-agents/usage#flags) on `run`, `sync`, or `watch`, or through the `telemetry` [configuration options](https://docs.specstory.com/integrations/terminal-coding-agents/usage#configuration). See the [OpenTelemetry](https://github.com/specstoryai/getspecstory/blob/dev/specstory-cli/README.md#open-telemetry) section of the README for more information.

   264â†’
   265â†’- Search across all your AI Chat history with the [SpecStory Cloud](https://cloud.specstory.com) web app
   266â†’- Automatically synchronize your Claude Code AI chat sessions with SpecStory Cloud, when logged in, using `specstory sync` and `specstory run`
   267â†’
   268â†’### ًں”§ CLI Configuration & Commands
   269â†’
   270â†’- Login to SpecStory Cloud using `specstory login`, logout using `specstory logout`
   271â†’- Skip cloud sync, when logged in, using the `--no-cloud-sync` flag
   272â†’
   273â†’### ًںگ› Bug Fixes
   274â†’
   275â†’- Don't create a new markdown file for a resumed or compacted session, append the resumed session to the existing markdown file
   276â†’- Fixed stray usage output during non-usage related errors
   277â†’- Fixed stray log output to stdout when running `specstory -v`, `specstory --version`, `specstory --help`, `specstory -h`, and invalid flags.
   278â†’
   279â†’
   280â†’## v0.8.0 2025-08-06
   281â†’
   282â†’### âڑ™ï¸ڈ Improvements
   283â†’
   284â†’- Skip writing markdown files for sessions that have no user messages, this is especially useful to avoid creating empty markdown files when using interactive mode (`specstory run`)
   285â†’
   286â†’### ًں”§ CLI Configuration & Commands
   287â†’
   288â†’- Fancy-schmancy new SpecStory logo that shows for `specstory` and  `specstory help`
   289â†’- Improved the output of `specstory sync`, to include showing the progress of parsing and processing JSONL files
   290â†’
   291â†’### ًںگ› Bug Fixes
   292â†’
   293â†’- Use of `/clear` command in Claude Code was resulting in subsequent sessions that were not being written to markdown files
   294â†’- Use of `/compact` or force-compacting the history in Claude Code was resulting in subsequent sessions that were not being written to markdown files
   295â†’- Fixed stray log output to stdout when running `specstory run`, `specstory version`, and `specstory help`
   296â†’
   297â†’
   298â†’## v0.7.0 2025-08-04
   299â†’
   300â†’### âڑ™ï¸ڈ Improvements
   301â†’
   302â†’- Creates a `.specstory/.project.json` file to track the project ID and name the project, in preparation for the future cloud sync feature in the CLI
   303â†’- Moved to structured logging with `log/slog`
   304â†’
   305â†’### âœڈï¸ڈ Markdown Enhancements
   306â†’
   307â†’- Uses the Claude Code provided summary of the session as the header of the markdown file, if available
   308â†’- Normalized the header formatting of markdown files to match the Cursor/VSC extension's markdown
   309â†’
   310â†’### ًں”§ CLI Configuration & Commands
   311â†’
   312â†’- Improved the explanation provided when `specstory sync` is run in a directory that is not an existing Claude Code project directory
   313â†’- `specstory check` now tells you where the `claude` command that SpecStory will use is located
   314â†’- New `--console` flag for the `run` and `sync` commands to enable error/warn/info debug output to stdout, replaces the `--verbose` flag
   315â†’- New `--debug` flag for the `run` and `sync` commands to enable debug level output to `--log` or `--console` output
   316â†’- Moved the log output (`--log` flag) to `.specstory/debug/debug.log` file
   317â†’- Improved the output of `specstory sync` to show the progress of parsing JSONL files and syncing markdown files
   318â†’
   319â†’

   324â†’- Support for the new "Native binary installation" of Claude Code (`curl -fsSL claude.ai/install.sh | bash`) which results in a `~/.local/bin/claude` binary

   364â†’## v0.4.0 2025-07-09
   365â†’
   366â†’### âœڈï¸ڈ Markdown Enhancements
   367â†’
   368â†’- Improved markdown formatting for agent tools use for "Bash", "Write", "Read" and "Grep"
   369â†’- Change attribution of chats from Claude Code to "Agent" rather than "Assistant"
   370â†’- Include the model and model version in the "Agent" attribution
   371â†’- Don't include the `isMeta`/`true` user messages "Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user.." in the markdown file
   372â†’- Good markdown formatting for `/` commands entered by the user in Claude Code
   373â†’- Better markdown file naming by skipping `isMeta` and `/` commands from the user message for naming the filename
   374â†’- Don't include the `` about malicious content in the generated markdown on "Read" tool results
   375â†’
   376â†’### ًں”§ CLI Configuration & Commands
   377â†’
   378â†’- Moved from a flag based CLI to one based on commands
   379â†’  - Commands for: `run`, `sync`, `check`, `help`, `version`
   380â†’  - Flags for: `--log`, `--verbose`, `--silent`, `--no-version-check`, `--no-usage-analytics`
   381â†’- Command specific help, e.g. `specstory help sync`
   382â†’- Fully styled help via [Fang](https://github.com/charmbracelet/fang)
   383â†’- Improved help text and example usage
   384â†’
   385â†’### ًںگ› Bug Fixes
   386â†’
   387â†’- Remove the proactive check for Claude Code in the PATH, as this no longer works with Anthropic's self-managed install approach (`~/.claude/local/claude` with a `claude` alias)
   388â†’- Instead of defaulting to `claude` when no Claude Code Command (`-c`) is provided, first check for the presence of `~/.claude/local/claude` and use that as the default if present, to work with Anthropic's self-managed install approach
   389â†’- If `~` is used in the Claude Code Command (`-c`) it was being treated literally, rather than expanded
```

Lines 18-19 (new): Added bug fix entry about fsnotify replacing polling

### pkg/providers/geminicli/path_utils.go

Lines 110-114 (modified): Updated `ResolveGeminiProjectDir` doc comment
```go
// ResolveGeminiProjectDir locates the Gemini project directory on disk.
// It tries three strategies in order:
//  1. Basename hint â€” ...
//  2. Hash-based â€” ...
//  3. Full scan â€” ...
```
- (5) âœ… Excellent doc comment â€” clearly describes the three-strategy resolution approach with priority ordering

Line 116 (modified): Renamed `projectDir` â†’ `hashDir`
- (6) âœ… Good rename â€” `hashDir` clearly communicates this is the SHA256-hash-based directory, distinguishing it from the new basename-based approach

Lines 123-127 (new): Resolve projectPath for `.project_root` matching
```go
resolvedProjectPath := projectPath
if resolvedProjectPath == "" {
    resolvedProjectPath, _ = osGetwd()
}
```
- (7) âڑ ï¸ڈ The error from `osGetwd()` is silently discarded. If `osGetwd()` fails, `resolvedProjectPath` stays empty, and we silently skip strategies 1 and 3 (both guarded by `if resolvedProjectPath != ""`). This is probably fine since `GetGeminiProjectDir` above already called `osGetwd()` successfully and would have errored first, but it's worth a comment explaining why the error is safe to ignore here.
- (8) âڑ ï¸ڈ There's duplicate `osGetwd()` resolution logic â€” `GetGeminiProjectDir` (line 81-87) also resolves an empty `projectPath` via `osGetwd()`. Consider whether `GetGeminiProjectDir` could return the resolved projectPath too, to avoid the second call.

Lines 145-151 (new): Strategy 1 â€” Basename hint
```go
if resolvedProjectPath != "" {
    canonicalProjectPath := canonicalizeProjectPath(resolvedProjectPath)
    if dir := findProjectDirByBasename(tmpDir, resolvedProjectPath, canonicalProjectPath); dir != "" {
        return dir, nil
    }
}
```
- (9) âœ… Good â€” tries the cheapest/most common strategy first
- (10) âœ… Properly guarded by `resolvedProjectPath != ""`

Lines 153-157 (new): Strategy 2 â€” Hash-based (legacy)
```go
if _, err := osStat(hashDir); err == nil {
    slog.Info("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Successfully resolved via hash directory", "hashDir", hashDir)
    return hashDir, nil
}
```
- (11) âœ… Clean fallback to legacy behavior â€” only checks if the hash directory exists
- (12) âڑ ï¸ڈ Non-`ErrNotExist` errors from `osStat` are silently treated as "not found." The old code had explicit handling for non-`ErrNotExist` errors. If `osStat` returns a permission error, for example, this silently falls through to strategy 3 and then to the "project_missing" error, which would be misleading. Consider checking `errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist)` specifically.

Lines 159-165 (new): Strategy 3 â€” Full scan
```go
if resolvedProjectPath != "" {
    if dir, scanErr := findProjectDirByProjectRoot(tmpDir, resolvedProjectPath); scanErr == nil && dir != "" {
        slog.Info("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Found project via .project_root scan", "dir", dir)
        return dir, nil
    }
}
```
- (13) âڑ ï¸ڈ If `scanErr != nil` (e.g., `ReadDir` fails), the error is silently swallowed and we fall through to a "project_missing" error. This hides potential filesystem issues. Consider logging `scanErr` at Warn level.

Lines 167-191 (new): Error path when no strategy succeeds
- (14) âœ… Good â€” retained the helpful `GeminiPathError` with known hashes for user diagnostics
- (15) âœ… Clean single exit point for the error case

Lines 194-211 (new): `matchesProjectRoot` function
```go
func matchesProjectRoot(dir, canonicalProjectPath string) bool {
```
- (16) âœ… Good function â€” encapsulates the `.project_root` file reading and comparison logic

- (18) âڑ ï¸ڈ The parameter name `canonicalProjectPath` is misleading. In `waitForProjectDir` (watcher.go:193), it's called with a canonicalized path. But in `findProjectDirByProjectRoot` (line 263), it's called with `canonicalProjectPath` from `canonicalizeProjectPath`. However, `findProjectDirByBasename` (line 237) also passes a properly canonicalized value. So the usage is correct, but it might be worth noting in the doc comment that the second parameter is expected to already be canonicalized, since the function name doesn't make this obvious.

Lines 213-223 (new): `canonicalizeProjectPath` helper

- (20) âڑ ï¸ڈ This function is called multiple times across the codebase for the same `projectPath`: in `ResolveGeminiProjectDir` (line 147), in `findProjectDirByProjectRoot` (line 250), and in `waitForProjectDir` (watcher.go:130). The callers already manage when to canonicalize, so this duplication of canonicalization across callers is slightly wasteful but not a real problem.

Lines 225-244 (new): `findProjectDirByBasename` function

Lines 246-271 (new): `findProjectDirByProjectRoot` function

- (25) âœ… Uses `os.ReadDir` which is more efficient than `os.ReadDir` + `Stat` individually
- (26) âڑ ï¸ڈ Uses `os.ReadDir` directly rather than through a mockable var. The rest of the file uses `osStat` for testability. This inconsistency is minor since the function is tested directly with real temp dirs, but worth noting.
- (27) â‌“ If there are multiple directories whose `.project_root` matches the same project path (theoretically shouldn't happen, but filesystem state can be surprising), this returns the first one found. Iteration order from `os.ReadDir` is alphabetical, so this is at least deterministic. Acceptable.

Lines 203-247 (new): `TestResolveGeminiProjectDirViaProjectRoot`

- (35) âœ… Uses real temp dir as `projectPath` so canonical path resolution works properly â€” this is a thoughtful design choice with an explanatory comment on line 213
- (36) âœ… Properly saves and restores `osGetwd`, `osUserHomeDir`, and `osStat` via `t.Cleanup`

The basename of the temp dir won't be `"my-project"`, so strategy 1 (basename hint) will miss. This means the test exercises strategy 3 (full scan), not strategy 1. This is fine â€” it's testing the fallback path â€” but the comment on line 238 says "via .project_root fallback" which correctly describes this. Good.

- (39) âœ… Good â€” `time` was only used by the polling-based `waitForDirectory`, now replaced

Lines 23-28 (removed): Removed `initialBackoff` and `maxBackoff` constants
- (40) âœ… Good cleanup â€” these polling constants are no longer needed with fsnotify

Lines 69-78 (modified): Renamed `projectDir` â†’ `hashDir` and added directory chain setup
```go

```
- (41) âœ… Good inline comments explaining what each variable resolves to
- (42) âœ… The chain of waits now starts from `~/.gemini` rather than `~/.gemini/tmp`, properly handling the case where `~/.gemini` doesn't exist yet

Lines 74-76 (new): Comment explaining the directory chain approach
```go
// Build the full directory chain: ~/.gemini â†’ tmp â†’ resolvedDir â†’ chats
// Each step's parent is guaranteed to exist by the previous step.
// $HOME (parent of geminiDir) always exists.
```
- (43) âœ… Excellent "why" comment â€” explains the sequential guarantee and the anchor point (`$HOME` always exists)

Lines 84-85 (modified): Changed `slog.Warn` â†’ `slog.Debug` for watcher stop messages
- (44) âœ… Good â€” context cancellation during shutdown is normal, not a warning

Lines 94-99 (new): `waitForProjectDir` call
```go
resolvedDir, err := waitForProjectDir(watcherCtx, tmpDir, projectPath, hashDir)
```
- (45) âœ… Good â€” this is the key change that enables watching for both legacy hash-based and new basename-based project directories

Lines 101-104 (modified): `chatsDir` now derived from `resolvedDir` instead of pre-computed
- (46) âœ… Correct â€” `chatsDir` must use the dynamically resolved directory

Lines 111, 115 (modified): Artifact watchers use `resolvedDir`
- (47) âœ… Consistent with the resolution change

Lines 123-205 (new): `waitForProjectDir` function
- (48) âœ… Well-documented doc comment with the three strategies
- (49) âœ… Good use of a `checkAll` closure to avoid duplicating the three-strategy check
- (50) âœ… Correct race-condition handling: checks once before adding watcher, adds watcher, re-checks to close the race window (line 170-173)
- (51) âڑ ï¸ڈ In the event loop (lines 175-204), when a `Create` event fires and `matchesProjectRoot` is called (line 193), there's a subtle timing issue: the `.project_root` file might not have been written yet when the directory is created. If Gemini CLI creates the directory first and writes `.project_root` shortly after, `matchesProjectRoot` could return false because the file doesn't exist yet. The hash-based check on line 188 is fine since it only checks the directory name. Consider whether this race is realistic â€” if Gemini CLI creates the directory atomically with `.project_root` inside it, it's fine. If not, a brief retry or a secondary watch for the `.project_root` file creation inside the new directory might be needed.
- (52) âœ… The `checkAll` function in the event handler provides a safety net â€” even if one event is missed, the next Create event in tmpDir will trigger a full re-check. However, the full re-check only runs for the two named strategies, not in response to any event â€” the `for` loop only checks for `Create` events and specifically checks `createdName == hashName` or `matchesProjectRoot`. A different event (non-Create) is skipped via `continue`. This is correct behavior.

Lines 330-387 (new): `waitForDirectoryFsnotify` function (replaces `waitForDirectory`)
- (53) âœ… Clean replacement â€” uses fsnotify instead of polling with exponential backoff
- (54) âœ… Proper race-condition handling: stat first, add watcher, re-stat after adding
- (55) âœ… Good error propagation â€” parent must exist (caller guarantees this via sequential wait chain)
- (56) âœ… `defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }()` â€” proper cleanup
- (57) âڑ ï¸ڈ Minor: the function signature uses `label string` without a doc comment on what `label` is for. The existing code already had this parameter, so not a regression, but it's used for logging clarity. Fine.

Lines 241-267 (removed): Old polling-based `waitForDirectory`
- (58) âœ… Cleanly removed â€” all callers updated to the new fsnotify version

---

## Top 5 Improvements

1. **Race condition in `waitForProjectDir` event handling (observation 51)**: When a new directory appears in tmpDir and `matchesProjectRoot` is called immediately, the `.project_root` file may not exist yet. Consider either: (a) after detecting a new directory that doesn't match hash, briefly watch that new directory for `.project_root` creation, or (b) call `checkAll()` instead of `matchesProjectRoot(event.Name, ...)` on each Create event, which would also catch directories that appeared between events.

2. **Silent error swallowing in strategy 2 and 3 of `ResolveGeminiProjectDir` (observations 12, 13)**: Non-`ErrNotExist` errors from `osStat(hashDir)` on line 154 and `scanErr` from `findProjectDirByProjectRoot` on line 161 are silently swallowed. At minimum, log them at Debug or Warn level so filesystem permission errors or other unexpected failures are visible.

3. **Duplicate `osGetwd()` resolution (observation 8)**: Both `GetGeminiProjectDir` (line 81-87) and `ResolveGeminiProjectDir` (line 124-127) independently resolve an empty `projectPath` via `osGetwd()`. This is a minor DRY concern â€” consider having `GetGeminiProjectDir` return the resolved path, or having `ResolveGeminiProjectDir` pass the resolved path explicitly.

4. **Changelog category for fsnotify change (observation 4)**: The polling-to-fsnotify change is categorized as a "Bug Fix" but is more accurately an improvement/optimization. The polling wasn't broken â€” it was just noisy and CPU-wasteful. Consider moving it to "Improvements."

5. **Missing test for basename hint strategy (observation 38)**: There's no integration-level test that exercises strategy 1 (basename hint) of `ResolveGeminiProjectDir`. `TestResolveGeminiProjectDirViaProjectRoot` only exercises strategy 3. Adding a test where the temp dir basename matches the `.project_root` directory name would verify the fast path works end-to-end.

---

## Test Assessment

### `TestFindProjectDirByProjectRoot` (lines 123-201)

- **Not trivial**: âœ… Tests our `.project_root` file reading, canonicalization, and matching logic

```text
   123â†’// waitForProjectDir waits for a Gemini project directory using three strategies:
   124â†’//  1. Basename hint â€” tmpDir/<project-basename> with matching .project_root
   125â†’//  2. Hash-based â€” the legacy hash directory
   126â†’//  3. Full .project_root scan â€” handles suffixed dirs like my-project-1
   127â†’//
   128â†’// If none exist yet, it watches tmpDir for new directory creation.
   129â†’func waitForProjectDir(ctx context.Context, tmpDir, projectPath, hashDir string) (string, error) {
   130â†’	canonicalProjectPath := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
   131â†’	hashName := filepath.Base(hashDir)
   132â†’
   133â†’	// checkAll runs all three strategies and returns the first match.
   134â†’	checkAll := func() string {
   135â†’		// 1. Basename hint
   136â†’		if dir := findProjectDirByBasename(tmpDir, projectPath, canonicalProjectPath); dir != "" {
   137â†’			return dir
   138â†’		}
   139â†’		// 2. Hash-based
   140â†’		if info, err := os.Stat(hashDir); err == nil && info.IsDir() {
   141â†’			slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: hash-based project directory ready", "path", hashDir)
   142â†’			return hashDir
   143â†’		}
   144â†’		// 3. Full scan
   145â†’		if match, err := findProjectDirByProjectRoot(tmpDir, projectPath); err == nil && match != "" {
   146â†’			return match
   147â†’		}
   148â†’		return ""
   149â†’	}
   150â†’
   151â†’	// Try existing directories first
   152â†’	if dir := checkAll(); dir != "" {
   153â†’		return dir, nil
   154â†’	}
   155â†’
   156â†’	// Nothing exists yet â€” watch tmpDir for new directory creation
   157â†’	watcher, err := fsnotify.NewWatcher()
   158â†’	if err != nil {
   159â†’		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to create fsnotify watcher for project dir: %w", err)
   160â†’	}
   161â†’	defer func() { _ = watcher.Close() }()
   162â†’
   163â†’	if err := watcher.Add(tmpDir); err != nil {
   164â†’		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to watch tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
   165â†’	}
   166â†’
   167â†’	slog.Debug("Gemini watcher: watching for project directory creation",
   168â†’		"tmpDir", tmpDir, "hashDir", hashName)
   169â†’
   170â†’	// Re-check after adding watcher to close the race window
   171â†’	if dir := checkAll(); dir != "" {
   172â†’		return dir, nil
   173â†’	}
   174â†’
   175â†’	for {
   176â†’		select {
   177â†’		case <-ctx.Done():
   178â†’			return "", ctx.Err()
   179â†’		case event, ok := <-watcher.Events:
   180â†’			if !ok {
   181â†’				return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify events channel closed for project dir watcher")
   182â†’			}
   183â†’			if !event.Has(fsnotify.Create) {
   184â†’				continue
   185â†’			}
   186â†’			// Re-run all strategies on any Create event rather than checking
   187â†’			// only the newly created entry. This handles the race where a
   188â†’			// directory was created moments ago but its .project_root file
   189â†’			// wasn't yet written when we last checked.
   190â†’			if dir := checkAll(); dir != "" {
   191â†’				return dir, nil
   192â†’			}
   193â†’		case err, ok := <-watcher.Errors:
   194â†’			if !ok {
   195â†’				return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify errors channel closed for project dir watcher")
   196â†’			}
   197â†’			return "", fmt.Errorf("fsnotify watcher error for project dir: %w", err)
   198â†’		}
   199â†’	}
   200â†’}
   201â†’
```

```text
   110â†’// ResolveGeminiProjectDir locates the Gemini project directory on disk.
   111â†’// It tries three strategies in order:
   112â†’//  1. Basename hint â€” tmpDir/<project-basename> with matching .project_root (fastest, most common for new CLI)
   113â†’//  2. Hash-based â€” tmpDir/<sha256-hash> (legacy Gemini CLI format)
   114â†’//  3. Full scan â€” read every .project_root in tmpDir (handles suffixed dirs like my-project-1)
   115â†’func ResolveGeminiProjectDir(projectPath string) (string, error) {
   116â†’	// Resolve projectPath once â€” used both for hash computation and .project_root matching
   117â†’	if projectPath == "" {
   118â†’		var err error
   119â†’		projectPath, err = osGetwd()
   120â†’		if err != nil {
   121â†’			return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to get current working directory: %v", err)
   122â†’		}
   123â†’	}
   124â†’
   125â†’	hashDir, err := GetGeminiProjectDir(projectPath)
   126â†’	if err != nil {
   127â†’		return "", err
   128â†’	}
   129â†’
   130â†’	tmpDir := filepath.Dir(hashDir)
   131â†’
   132â†’	slog.Debug("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Checking for Gemini directories",
   133â†’		"tmpDir", tmpDir,
   134â†’		"hashDir", hashDir)
   135â†’
   136â†’	if _, err := osStat(tmpDir); err != nil {
   137â†’		if errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
   138â†’			slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Gemini tmp directory not found", "tmpDir", tmpDir)
   139â†’			return "", &GeminiPathError{
   140â†’				Kind:    "tmp_missing",
   141â†’				Path:    tmpDir,
   142â†’				Message: fmt.Sprintf("Gemini tmp directory %q not found. Run the Gemini CLI at least once or verify ~/.gemini exists.", tmpDir),
   143â†’			}
   144â†’		}
   145â†’		return "", fmt.Errorf("failed to read Gemini tmp directory %q: %w", tmpDir, err)
   146â†’	}
   147â†’
   148â†’	// 1. Basename hint â€” check tmpDir/<basename> with .project_root match
   149â†’	canonicalProjectPath := canonicalizeProjectPath(projectPath)
   150â†’	if dir := findProjectDirByBasename(tmpDir, projectPath, canonicalProjectPath); dir != "" {
   151â†’		return dir, nil
   152â†’	}
   153â†’
   154â†’	// 2. Hash-based â€” legacy Gemini CLI format
   155â†’	if _, err := osStat(hashDir); err == nil {
   156â†’		slog.Info("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Successfully resolved via hash directory", "hashDir", hashDir)
   157â†’		return hashDir, nil
   158â†’	} else if !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist) {
   159â†’		slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Unexpected error checking hash directory",
   160â†’			"hashDir", hashDir, "error", err)
   161â†’	}
   162â†’
   163â†’	// 3. Full scan â€” handles suffixed dirs like my-project-1
   164â†’	if dir, scanErr := findProjectDirByProjectRoot(tmpDir, projectPath); scanErr != nil {
   165â†’		slog.Warn("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Error during .project_root scan",
   166â†’			"tmpDir", tmpDir, "error", scanErr)
   167â†’	} else if dir != "" {
   168â†’		slog.Info("ResolveGeminiProjectDir: Found project via .project_root scan", "dir", dir)
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-02 23:43:05Z)**_

All 6 observations addressed. Here's a summary of what changed:

**#8 â€” Duplicate `osGetwd()` resolution**: Moved `projectPath` resolution to the top of `ResolveGeminiProjectDir` (lines 116-123), before `GetGeminiProjectDir` is called. Now `projectPath` is resolved exactly once and passed as non-empty to `GetGeminiProjectDir`, which skips its own `osGetwd()` call. Also removed the `resolvedProjectPath` variable and the guard clauses around strategies 1 and 3 (no longer needed since `projectPath` is guaranteed non-empty).

**#12 â€” Silent error swallowing in strategy 2**: Added an `else if !errors.Is(err, os.ErrNotExist)` branch (lines 158-161) that logs unexpected errors (e.g., permission errors) at Warn level instead of silently treating them as "not found."

**#13 â€” Silent error swallowing in strategy 3**: Restructured the strategy 3 block (lines 164-170) to check `scanErr != nil` first and log it at Warn level, rather than silently falling through.

**#18 â€” `matchesProjectRoot` param documentation**: Expanded the doc comment (lines 199-202) to explicitly state that `canonicalProjectPath` must already be in canonical form, referencing the helper functions that produce it.

**#51 â€” Race condition in `waitForProjectDir`**: Replaced the specific `matchesProjectRoot` check on Create events with a `checkAll()` call (lines 186-192). Now any Create event in tmpDir triggers a full re-scan of all three strategies, which catches directories whose `.project_root` file wasn't yet written when the directory first appeared.

**#57 â€” Missing `label` param description**: Added description of the `label` parameter to the `waitForDirectoryFsnotify` doc comment (line 333).
